texto: But if the Busyness = Status equation works, it’s strictly because a social game has emerged wherein relatively privileged, educated people with all kinds of Choice disguise their decisions to be busy as manifestations of the universe’s insatiable demand for their particular prowess. And the rest of us agree to be impressed. It’s a stupid game. We can stop any time.
explicacao: Citação de Tasha Golden sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “But if the Busyness = Status equation works, it’s strictly because a social game has emerged wher…” articula reflexao, busyness, quality-of-life em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e quality-of-life.

frase_original: But if the Busyness = Status equation works, it’s strictly because a social game has emerged wherein relatively privileged, educated people with all kinds of Choice disguise their decisions to be busy as manifestations of the universe’s insatiable demand for their particular prowess. And the rest of us agree to be impressed. It’s a stupid game. We can stop any time.

texto: There has to be something other than keeping busy that gives human life value, because our society so often functions as if ‘productivity’ and/or ‘usefulness’ are the measures of human value, and — particularly for artists — this is deeply problematic. We shouldn’t given in.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tasha Golden: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “There has to be something other than keeping busy that gives human life value, because our societ…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, creative-life, quality-of-life pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de quality-of-life e writing-life.
